# Rainfall Escalation Policy

## Rainfall pressure levels

When forecast or observed 7-day rainfall exceeds 120 mm, planners should move the location into watch status and inspect drainage constraints. When rainfall exceeds 180 mm or the river signal is severe, planners should prepare a district action brief and coordinate field verification.

## River and drainage signals

River pressure should be reviewed together with low elevation, blocked canals, poor road access, and historical flooding. A single rainfall number should not trigger an official evacuation order without field verification.

## Communication boundary

Public warnings must come from the responsible authority. FloodLens can recommend review, field verification, resource staging, and report preparation.
